articles.title: From Ahinda to Mekedatu: 5 challenges before Karnataka's new CM
articles.description: Karnataka's new Congress government, led by DK Shivakumar, faces a critical transition. Key challenges include cabinet formation balancing caste and regional demands, sustaining welfare guarantees amid fiscal strain, and navigating the sensitive Mekedatu reservoir dispute with Tamil Nadu. Shivakumar must also retain the crucial Ahinda support base and ensure development extends beyond Bengaluru to rural areas.
articles.full_text: Manu Aiyappa has been with The Times of India for over two decades, currently serving as the Political Editor. His reporting focuses on politics, public policy, and in-depth features that aim to inform and engage a broad readership. Over the years, his work has been recognised by both the Press Club of Bangalore and the Karnataka Media Academy for its clarity, credibility, and relevance to public discourse. Read More

articles.title: Is Indus Treaty deadlock hurting Pak? Karachi reels under chronic water shortage
articles.description: Karachi faces a severe water crisis during peak summer, coinciding with Eid-ul-Adha. Jamaat-e-Islami Pakistan chief Hafiz Naeem ur Rehman has accused the ruling PPP of gross mismanagement and failure to address the city's chronic water shortages despite 18 years in power.  Nearly 70% of the city is reportedly experiencing extended supply disruptions, forcing residents to buy expensive private tankers.

articles.title: 'If namaz on road wrong, ban all festivals': Owaisi points at 'double standard'
articles.description: AIMIM chief Asaduddin Owaisi criticized what he termed a double standard regarding religious practices, stating that if offering namaz on roads is deemed wrong, then restrictions should apply equally to all faiths, citing Article 25 of the Constitution. He questioned why objections are raised against Muslim prayers but not religious processions of other communities.

articles.title: 'They walk among us': White House launches alien-themed website to track immigrants
articles.description: The White House launched a new space-themed website, Aliens.gov, to highlight immigration enforcement actions. Using extraterrestrial imagery, the site aims to showcase arrests of undocumented immigrants and promote President Trump's agenda. It features a live dashboard and interactive map of enforcement activities, drawing criticism for its dehumanizing language.

articles.title: Gulf's other war: UAE carried out strikes on Iran despite ceasefire — Report
articles.description: The UAE's involvement in the Middle East conflict was deeper than previously known, with coordinated strikes on Iran, including energy facilities, even after an April ceasefire. These actions exposed divisions within the Gulf, as Saudi Arabia expressed concerns about escalating risks to regional energy supplies and global markets.

articles.title: China building launch pads near nuclear missile silos
Is IShowSpeed working on a FIFA World Cup 2026 song? Behind-the-scenes footage sparks major fan speculation
articles.description: Behind-the-scenes footage has sparked speculation that IShowSpeed may be working on a FIFA World Cup 2026 song. Videos from a large-scale shoot in Miami showed the streamer surrounded by production crews, drones, flags, and football-themed visuals. Fans linked the project to the upcoming tournament, especially after Speed hinted at World Cup plans during a recent tour. The rumors have gained traction because his 2022 World Cup anthem became a viral hit, amassing more than 208 million views on YouTube.

articles.title: ODI tickets worth Rs 52.36 lakh went unsold: Khanna
articles.description: "Tickets worth Rupees 52,36,000 were unsold. Why all the tickets were not sold-out? DDCA started online ticket sale quite late that too without proper advertisement," Khanna claimed in a media release.

articles.title: BRO recruitment 2026: Applications open for 899 electrician and other posts: Check complete details here
articles.description: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) has begun the recruitment process for 899 vacancies, including Electrician and several other posts. Candidates can submit applications online until July 4, 2026. The selection process includes a written examination, Physical Efficiency Test, Trade Test, and medical examination, with final merit determined by written exam performance.

articles.description: Maharashtra's Class 11 admissions are underway with the release of the FYJC Merit List 2026. articles.title: 'Cannot continue indefinitely': Supreme Court extends TET deadline till August 2028
articles.description: The Supreme Court mandated that in-service teachers must pass the Teacher Eligibility Test (TET) to continue their employment, extending the deadline to August 31, 2028. Dismissing review petitions, the bench emphasized the RTE Act's intent for all teachers to meet minimum qualifications, prioritizing children's educational future.
articles.full_text: The Supreme Court mandated that in-service teachers must pass the Teacher Eligibility Test (TET) to continue their employment, extending the deadline to August 31, 2028. Dismissing review petitions, the bench emphasized the RTE Act's intent for all teachers to meet minimum qualifications, prioritizing children's educational future.

articles.title: Marcia Lucas passes away at 80
articles.description: Acclaimed editor Marcia Lucas, the creative force behind iconic films like 'Star Wars' and 'American Graffiti', has passed away at 80. Her family confirmed her peaceful death from cancer in Rancho Mirage, California. Remembered as a trailblazer and brilliant storyteller, Lucas's indelible mark on cinema will be celebrated, alongside her warmth and humor by those who knew her.

articles.title: Joan Cusack stuns at 'Toy Story 5' premiere after 11-year hiatus
articles.description: Actress Joan Cusack graced the 'Toy Story 5' premiere in London, marking her first red carpet appearance in over a decade. She looked stunning in a black and white ensemble, joining co-stars Tom Hanks and Tim Allen. Cusack, who voiced Jessie, had largely stepped away from Hollywood after her 2015 Emmy win to focus on family life in Chicago.

articles.description: In response to fan feedback about obstructed views and concert layout challenges, Harry Styles' team is taking meaningful steps to enhance the concert experience. Understanding the commitment fans show through their ticket purchases, they are instating improvements for the forthcoming 'Together, Together' tour dates to ensure a memorable and inclusive atmosphere for everyone attending.

articles.title: Reema Kagti on nepotism debate: 'People outrage, but only...'
articles.description: Filmmaker Reema Kagti highlights a disconnect: audiences decry nepotism but often favor established stars over new talent. She argues for 'patronage' over mere 'support' for original content, emphasizing that art thrives on appreciation. Kagti calls for introspection from both creators and viewers to foster a healthier film ecosystem, particularly for under-represented stories like those from the Northeast. Read on to know more in detail.
